name: v0.3 — Real End-to-End Usage: Settings Cleanup, Add-Appliance UI, and Schedule Applier
description: Five focused stories to take v0.2 (HACS Integration with sign-in working) to v0.3 (a user can actually register an appliance, see the schedule, and have it applied to their thermostat). Driven by real install feedback: the Settings tab fields don't match HVAC vocabulary (asks for 'Indoor / Outdoor / Power' sensors when the thermostat exposes 'ambient / target / state' and outdoor temp comes from a weather entity); selections don't visibly persist (no Save button); pricing zones show as bare numbers ('Zone 1') with no utility/region context; the 'Add appliance' button is a dead button (no @click handler); and the integration never actually writes setpoints to the climate entity (the panel reads the schedule but the thermostat is unaffected). Stories 1–3 are Settings UX cleanup. Story 4 wires the appliance-create flow. Story 5 makes the integration apply the schedule throughout the day. Two completed stories from the prior batch (US-FE-SWEEP-01, US-FE-CHART-OVERLAY-01) are kept at the bottom for historical reference, with priority 99 so RALPH skips them.
env_policy: Mixed batch: stories 1–4 are frontend (vitest + happy-dom + mocked fetch). Story 5 adds Python in custom_components/hungry_machines/ with pytest + unittest.mock. No live API, no live HA, no real Supabase auth in any Verify step. Story 5's auth model: the integration's config flow asks for the user's hungrymachines.io email + password (the same credentials they use on the website and in the panel). The integration POSTs them to api.hungrymachines.io/auth/login (the same endpoint src/api/auth.ts uses), receives access_token + refresh_token, and stores ONLY the tokens in entry.data — never the password. Tests mock the login + refresh + schedules endpoints and never make a real network call. All Verify steps run offline via npm run build, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, python -m py_compile, and pytest with mocked aiohttp + mocked hass.services.async_call.

stories.title: Replace entity fields: drop indoor/outdoor/power, add climate + weather
stories.description: As a user opening the panel's Settings tab, I want fields that match how Home Assistant actually exposes my thermostat — a single climate entity (which carries ambient + target + state) and a weather entity (for outdoor temperature) — instead of four separate sensors that don't map cleanly to any real device.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts`, replace the existing `EntityField` literal-union type and the `ENTITY_FIELDS` array. New `EntityField = 'climate' | 'weather'`. New `ENTITY_FIELDS = [{ key: 'climate', label: 'HVAC climate entity', domain: 'climate' }, { key: 'weather', label: 'Weather', domain: 'weather' }]`. The `EntityMap` type in `src/store.ts` follows: `{ climate?: string; weather?: string }`.

Update `src/store.ts` `getEntityMap()` to silently drop legacy keys (`indoor_temp`, `outdoor_temp`, `power`) when reading from localStorage, and to write back the cleaned-up shape. This is a one-time migration — users who had the old four-field map should see their non-applicable selections quietly removed without an error or warning.

Where the panel currently passes the entity map to cards (search for `entityMap`/`indoor_temp`/`outdoor_temp` callers), update consumers: thermostat-card and savings-card no longer receive `indoor_temp` / `outdoor_temp` / `power` keys. If any card UI reads these via the entity map (rather than via card-level config), update it to read from the climate or weather entity's attributes instead — `climate.*` exposes `current_temperature` and `temperature` (target) under `attributes`; `weather.*` exposes `temperature` (current outdoor) under `attributes`.

Update `tests/settings.test.ts`: assert exactly two entity-domain selects render (`climate` and `weather`); assert no select with id/name containing `indoor_temp`, `outdoor_temp`, or `power` renders; assert that `getEntityMap()` returning a legacy `{ indoor_temp: 'sensor.foo', power: 'sensor.bar' }` is filtered to `{}` (or whatever subset is still valid) on read.

Update `tests/store.test.ts`: add a case where localStorage starts with the legacy four-key shape and asserts hydration produces a clean two-key (or empty) shape, AND that the next `setEntityMap` write persists only the new shape.

Verify: `npm test` passes (settings + store tests green, no regression elsewhere); `npm run build` succeeds; `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean; `grep -nE "'indoor_temp'|'outdoor_temp'|'power'" src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts src/store.ts` returns no matches inside the field-list / type definitions (legacy-key migration code may mention them as string literals in `delete` statements — that's expected); `grep -cE "'climate'|'weather'" src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts` returns at least 2.

stories.title: Settings tab: explicit Save / Reset buttons with visible confirmation
stories.description: As a user changing my entity selections or pricing zone, I want explicit Save and Reset buttons with a visible 'Saved' confirmation — so I know my choices are committed and not silently discarded. Today the form auto-persists on every change (which works) but gives no feedback, leading users to wonder if anything happened.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts`, change `_onEntityChange` and the equivalent zone change handler to update only an in-memory draft state (`_entityMapDraft: EntityMap`, `_zoneDraft: number`). Do NOT call `setEntityMap` or any zone persistence helper inside the change handlers. The Save button is the only persistence path.

Add a 'Save' button below the Settings form. Clicking it calls `setEntityMap(this._entityMapDraft)` (and the equivalent zone persistence call), then sets `_savedFlash = true` for 2 seconds (use `setTimeout` cleared on disconnect) which renders 'Saved' inline next to the button. Disabled when there are no unsaved changes (i.e. when draft equals last persisted state).

Add a 'Reset' button next to Save. Clicking it sets `_entityMapDraft = getEntityMap()` and `_zoneDraft = <current persisted zone>`, reverting any pending edits. Disabled when there are no unsaved changes.

On `connectedCallback` (or wherever the panel currently hydrates state), seed both `_entityMap` AND `_entityMapDraft` from `getEntityMap()`. After successful save, also update `_entityMap` (the 'last saved' baseline) to the just-saved value so Save/Reset disable correctly.

Update `tests/settings.test.ts`: case A — selecting a climate entity does NOT call `localStorage.setItem` (verified via spy on the store's `safeSet` or by reading the localStorage mock); case B — clicking Save DOES call the persistence, AND a 'Saved' text node appears in the form; case C — after Save, click Reset; assert form reverts to the saved state (not to the draft state); case D — after Save with no further edits, both Save and Reset are disabled (assert via `disabled` attribute on the buttons); case E — re-mounting the panel (simulating page reload) hydrates the form fields back to the saved values.

Verify: `npm test` passes (all settings tests green, panel tests still green); `npm run build` succeeds; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `grep -cE 'class="save-btn"|class="reset-btn"' src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts` returns at least 2; `grep -c 'Saved' src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts` returns at least 1; `grep -c '_entityMapDraft' src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts` returns at least 4 (declared + read + written by handlers + reset).

stories.title: Pricing zone dropdown shows utility + region labels
stories.description: As a user picking my pricing zone, I want to see 'SDG&E — San Diego' instead of bare 'Zone 1' — so I can choose the right plan without consulting a separate doc. Today the dropdown shows the eight preset zones as numbers only, which forces users to guess.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/pricing-zones.ts` exporting a typed const `PRICING_ZONE_LABELS: Record<1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7 | 8, { provider: string; region: string }>`. Populate with the eight presets the backend supports (per CLAUDE.md / hm-backend-structure.md): zones 1–8 cover SDG&E, ConEd, Xcel, etc. Use the exact mapping documented in the API repo's `app/services/pricing.py` if accessible; otherwise hard-code reasonable defaults (zone 1 = 'SDG&E' / 'San Diego', zone 2 = 'ConEd' / 'New York City', zones 3–8 = 'Provider TBD' / 'Region TBD' as placeholders) and add a TODO comment with the file path that needs reconciling. The point is to get useful labels in front of users now, not to enumerate all eight perfectly.

In `src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts`, the existing `PRICING_ZONES` const and the zone `<option>` rendering must use the new label data. Each option text is `${provider} — ${region} (Zone ${n})`. The underlying value submitted to the API stays the integer 1–8; only the display text changes.

Add a small `<span class="zone-hint">` under the zone select that shows the currently-selected zone's full label. Updates reactively when the user changes selection (drives off `_zoneDraft` from US-FE-SETTINGS-SAVE-01 if that landed first; otherwise drives off the pre-existing zone state).

Update `tests/settings.test.ts` (or `tests/settings-rates.test.ts` if that's where zone UI lives): assert that the zone select renders 8 options; assert that option for value `1` has text matching the regex `SDG.?E.*San Diego`; assert that the zone-hint span updates when a different option is selected.

Add `tests/pricing-zones.test.ts` with two cases: (a) `PRICING_ZONE_LABELS[1].provider` matches `/SDG.?E/`; (b) the keys `1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8` are all present (assert `Object.keys(PRICING_ZONE_LABELS).length === 8`).

Verify: `npm test` passes (new and existing tests green); `npm run build` succeeds; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `grep -c 'PRICING_ZONE_LABELS' src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts src/data/pricing-zones.ts` returns at least 2; `grep -oE 'SDG.?E' src/data/pricing-zones.ts | head -1` matches.

stories.title: Add appliance modal: type picker + per-type config form
stories.description: As a new user with no appliances registered, I want to click the 'Add appliance' button on the empty dashboard and fill out a form to register an HVAC, EV charger, home battery, or water heater — so I can start receiving optimized schedules without using the API directly. Today the button has no @click handler and is a dead button.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/ui/appliance-form.ts` exporting `HmApplianceForm` (LitElement). Reactive properties: `open: boolean` (controls modal visibility), `submitting: boolean` (disables form during submit), `error: string | null` (inline error message). Emits `appliance-created` (`CustomEvent` with `detail: { appliance: Appliance }`) on successful submit and `cancelled` on cancel/close. Two-step UI inside the modal: step 1 picks the appliance type (HVAC / EV charger / Home battery / Water heater); step 2 collects name + per-type config and submits.

Step 1 — render four buttons (or cards) labeled per the existing `TYPE_LABELS` mapping in `hungry-machines-panel.ts` (HVAC, EV, Battery, Water). Picking one sets internal state `_pickedType: ApplianceType` and advances to step 2. A 'Back' button on step 2 returns to step 1 and clears any unsaved input.

Step 2 — common 'Name' text input (required, non-empty after trim). Per-type config inputs: HVAC — `<select>` for `hvac_type` with options `central_ac | window_ac | heat_pump | furnace`, plus `<input type="number" min="100" step="10">` for `home_size_sqft`. EV charger — three number inputs: `battery_capacity_kwh` (min 1, step 0.1), `max_charge_rate_kw` (min 0.5, step 0.1), `efficiency` (min 0.5, max 1.0, step 0.01, default 0.9). Home battery — `capacity_kwh`, `max_charge_rate_kw`, `max_discharge_rate_kw` (all positive, step 0.1). Water heater — `tank_size_gallons` (positive integer), `element_watts` (positive integer), `insulation_factor` (min 0, max 1, step 0.05, default 0.5). Disable the Submit button while any required field is empty or invalid.

Add `create()` wrapper to `src/api/appliances.ts`: `async create(input: { appliance_type: ApplianceType; name: string; config: Record<string, unknown> }): Promise<Appliance>` — POSTs to `/api/v1/appliances` via `apiFetch`, returns the created appliance object. Add a corresponding type for the request body if not already present.

On submit, the form calls `appliancesApi.create(...)`. On 2xx: dispatch `appliance-created` with the created appliance, set `open=false`, reset internal state. On 4xx/5xx: set `error` to the server's message (or 'Could not create appliance — please try again' as fallback) and keep the form open with inputs preserved.

Wire the existing `<button class="add-btn" type="button">Add appliance</button>` at line ~835 of `hungry-machines-panel.ts` to a `@click` handler that sets a new reactive state field `_addApplianceOpen = true` and renders `<hm-appliance-form .open=${this._addApplianceOpen} @appliance-created=${this._onApplianceCreated} @cancelled=${this._onApplianceCancelled}>`. Add a second 'Add another appliance' button at the bottom of the populated dashboard (when there's at least one appliance) so users can register more after the first.

On `appliance-created`: close the modal, then re-fetch `/api/v1/schedules` (and `/api/v1/appliances` if used directly) so the new card appears in the dashboard. The newly-added appliance might not have a schedule yet (since the optimizer runs nightly) — show its card with a 'Schedule will appear after tomorrow's optimization' placeholder text instead of the chart in that case.
Register `hm-appliance-form` in `src/main.ts` with the same `customElements.get` idempotency guard as the other elements.

New tests in `tests/appliance-form.test.ts`: (a) initial render shows step 1 with four type buttons; (b) clicking 'HVAC' advances to step 2 with the HVAC-specific fields visible; (c) submit with empty name does NOT call fetch; (d) successful submit (mock 200) calls `fetch` with `POST /api/v1/appliances` and a body whose `appliance_type` matches the picked type and `config` contains the entered values, dispatches `appliance-created`, and sets `open=false`; (e) failed submit (mock 500) renders the error message and leaves `open=true`; (f) clicking 'Cancel' on step 2 dispatches `cancelled` and the form is reset to step 1.

Extend `tests/panel.test.ts`: clicking the 'Add appliance' button in the empty-dashboard state opens the modal (assert `<hm-appliance-form open="true">` is present in the rendered tree); after dispatching `appliance-created` from the form, the panel re-fetches `/api/v1/schedules` (assert via spy on the fetch mock).

Verify: `npm test` passes (new test files green, no regression); `npm run build` succeeds; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `grep -nE '@click' src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts | grep -i 'add' | wc -l` returns at least 1; `grep -c 'hm-appliance-form' src/main.ts src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts` returns at least 2; `grep -c 'appliancesApi.create\|appliances.create' src/ui/appliance-form.ts` returns at least 1.

stories.title: Python schedule applier with email/password auth: fetch nightly, apply on each 30-min boundary
stories.description: As a user with an HVAC appliance registered, I want to enter my hungrymachines.io email and password during integration setup — the same credentials I use on the website and in the panel — so the integration can authenticate as me, fetch my optimized schedule once after the nightly optimization runs, and write the targets to my thermostat throughout the day. Today the panel shows a beautiful schedule but the thermostat is unaffected, so the optimization has no effect on the user's home.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `custom_components/hungry_machines/auth.py` with three async functions, all using `aiohttp_client.async_get_clientsession(hass)` and the constant `API_BASE_URL = 'https://api.hungrymachines.io'` from `const.py`. (1) `async login(hass, email, password) -> dict | None` — POSTs `{API_BASE_URL}/auth/login` with JSON body `{email, password}`. On 200 returns `{'access_token': str, 'refresh_token': str, 'expires_at': float, 'user_id': str, 'email': str}` where `expires_at = time.time() + (response.expires_in or 3600) - 60` (60-second safety margin). On 4xx/5xx returns None and logs warning. Network errors caught and logged, return None. (2) `async refresh(hass, refresh_token) -> dict | None` — POSTs `{API_BASE_URL}/auth/refresh` with JSON body `{refresh_token}`. Same return shape and error handling as login. (3) `async current_token(hass, entry) -> str | None` — checks `entry.data.get('expires_at', 0) > time.time() + 30` (30s margin); if so returns `entry.data['access_token']` directly with no network call. Otherwise calls `refresh(hass, entry.data['refresh_token'])`; on success persists new tokens via `hass.config_entries.async_update_entry(entry, data={**entry.data, 'access_token': new.access_token, 'refresh_token': new.refresh_token, 'expires_at': new.expires_at})` and returns the new access_token. On refresh failure returns None — caller is responsible for triggering reauth.

Update `custom_components/hungry_machines/config_flow.py` initial user step to ask for three fields: `email` (TextSelector with `type='email'`), `password` (TextSelector with `type='password'`), `climate_entity` (EntitySelector with `domain='climate'`; if EntitySelector is unavailable for the running HA version, use a `vol.In(hass.states.async_entity_ids('climate'))` fallback). On submit: call `auth.login(hass, email, password)`. On success: store `{email, access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, climate_entity}` in `entry.data` (NEVER store the password) and call `self.async_set_unique_id(returned_user_id)` followed by `self._abort_if_unique_id_configured()`. On failure: re-render the form with `errors={'base': 'invalid_auth'}`. Update `strings.json` and `translations/en.json`: add `'invalid_auth': 'Invalid email or password — please try again.'` to the `error` block, and a step description that reads 'Enter your hungrymachines.io credentials. We use these to fetch your optimized schedules and apply them to your thermostat. Your password is sent once to api.hungrymachines.io to obtain access tokens; only the tokens are stored locally in Home Assistant.'

Add `async_step_reauth` and `async_step_reauth_confirm` to the same config flow class (HA-standard reauth pattern). Triggered when token refresh fails. The reauth_confirm step pre-fills the email from `entry.data['email']`, asks for password only, and on success updates `entry.data` with new tokens via `hass.config_entries.async_update_entry`. Add a `async_step_reauth` test stub that mocks the trigger.

Add `OptionsFlow` to the config flow class. Single step that lets the user change the `climate_entity` (no token rotation needed — credentials are not in options). Stores in `entry.options`. Update strings/translations with `'options': {'step': {'init': {'title': 'Hungry Machines settings', 'description': 'Change which climate entity receives the optimized schedule.'}}}`.

Create `custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py` with two async functions, both calling `auth.current_token(hass, entry)` for the bearer token. (1) `async fetch_today_schedule(hass, entry) -> dict | None` — gets token via `current_token`; if None, calls `entry.async_start_reauth(hass)` and returns None. Otherwise GETs `{API_BASE_URL}/api/v1/schedules` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. On 401 (token rejected even after refresh): clears `hass.data['hungry_machines']['schedule']`, calls `entry.async_start_reauth(hass)`, returns None. On 200: extracts the appliance entry where `appliance_type == 'hvac'`, stores `{'high_temps': [...48], 'low_temps': [...48], 'fetched_at': <iso8601>}` in `hass.data['hungry_machines']['schedule']`, returns the cached dict. On other failures (5xx, network error, malformed body): logs warning and returns None without disturbing any cached schedule. (2) `async apply_current_slot(hass, entry) -> None` — reads `hass.data['hungry_machines']['schedule']`. If absent, logs info 'no schedule cached, skipping apply' and returns. Computes `slot = (now.hour * 2) + (1 if now.minute >= 30 else 0)` from local time. Reads `climate_entity = entry.options.get('climate_entity') or entry.data.get('climate_entity')`; if falsy, logs info and returns. Calls `await hass.services.async_call('climate', 'set_temperature', {'entity_id': climate_entity, 'target_temp_low': low_temps[slot], 'target_temp_high': high_temps[slot]}, blocking=False)`. Catches and logs any exception at warning level so a single bad climate entity doesn't bring the integration down.

Wire the scheduler into `custom_components/hungry_machines/__init__.py` `async_setup_entry`. After the existing panel registration code: (a) `hass.data.setdefault('hungry_machines', {})`; (b) `await fetch_today_schedule(hass, entry)` once at setup; (c) register `async_track_time_change(hass, callback, hour=5, minute=5, second=0)` that calls `fetch_today_schedule(hass, entry)`; (d) register `async_track_time_change(hass, callback, minute=[0, 30], second=0)` that calls `apply_current_slot(hass, entry)`. Store the unsubscribe callbacks on `hass.data['hungry_machines'][entry.entry_id]['unsub']` (a list) so `async_unload_entry` can clean them up. Update `async_unload_entry` to call every stored unsubscribe callback BEFORE `async_remove_panel(hass, PANEL_URL_PATH)`.

Update `custom_components/hungry_machines/const.py` to add `API_BASE_URL = 'https://api.hungrymachines.io'`. The integration must not hardcode this URL anywhere else — every HTTP call goes through the constant.
Bump version to `0.3.0` in BOTH `custom_components/hungry_machines/manifest.json` AND `package.json`.

Update `README.md`: between the existing 'Add the integration' step and the 'Sign in' step, replace any prior token-paste content with a clear explanation that the integration's setup flow asks for the user's hungrymachines.io email + password (the same credentials used on the website and in the panel) plus their HVAC climate entity. Note that the password is sent once to api.hungrymachines.io to obtain access tokens, and only the tokens are persisted locally in Home Assistant.

Create `custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/__init__.py` (empty file to make it a package) and `custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/test_auth.py` with pytest + unittest.mock cases. Mock `aiohttp_client.async_get_clientsession` to return a session whose `post` returns mocked responses. Use `MagicMock` for HomeAssistant and ConfigEntry. Cases: (a) `login` with mocked 200 returns dict containing access_token, refresh_token, expires_at; (b) `login` with mocked 401 returns None and logs warning (assert via `caplog`); (c) `login` with `aiohttp.ClientError` returns None; (d) `refresh` with mocked 200 returns new tokens; (e) `current_token` with `expires_at = time.time() + 600` returns `entry.data['access_token']` directly and does NOT call refresh (assert via `assert_not_called` on the refresh mock); (f) `current_token` with `expires_at` in the past calls `refresh` and updates entry via `hass.config_entries.async_update_entry`; (g) `current_token` when refresh returns None returns None.

Create `custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/test_scheduler.py` with cases that mock `auth.current_token` directly. Cases: (a) `fetch_today_schedule` with `current_token` returning a valid token and a mocked 200 schedules response populates `hass.data['hungry_machines']['schedule']` with the right shape; (b) `fetch_today_schedule` with `current_token` returning None calls `entry.async_start_reauth` and returns None (assert via spy); (c) `fetch_today_schedule` with a mocked 401 response also calls `async_start_reauth` and returns None; (d) `apply_current_slot` with a cached schedule and a configured `climate.living_room` entity calls `hass.services.async_call('climate', 'set_temperature', ...)` exactly once with the correct slot's targets; (e) `apply_current_slot` with no cached schedule is a no-op; (f) `apply_current_slot` with a cache but no `climate_entity` configured is a no-op.

Create `custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/test_config_flow.py` with cases: (a) the initial user step with a valid email + password + climate entity (mock `auth.login` returning a successful tokens dict) creates a config entry whose `data` contains email, access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, climate_entity but NOT password; (b) initial user step with mock `auth.login` returning None re-renders the form with `errors['base'] == 'invalid_auth'` and does NOT create an entry; (c) reauth_confirm step with mock `auth.login` returning new tokens calls `hass.config_entries.async_update_entry` with the new tokens; (d) options flow lets the user change `climate_entity` and persists it to `entry.options`.

Verify: `cd /home/gng/Documents/Encephalon/Hungry\ Machines/hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm test` passes (frontend unaffected); `npm run build` succeeds; `python3 -m py_compile custom_components/hungry_machines/__init__.py custom_components/hungry_machines/auth.py custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py custom_components/hungry_machines/config_flow.py custom_components/hungry_machines/const.py` exits 0; `python3 -m pytest custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/ -v` passes (every auth, scheduler, and config-flow case green); `grep -c '0.3.0' custom_components/hungry_machines/manifest.json package.json` returns at least 2; `grep -c 'API_BASE_URL\|fetch_today_schedule\|apply_current_slot\|current_token' custom_components/hungry_machines/auth.py custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py custom_components/hungry_machines/__init__.py` returns at least 4; `grep -c 'password' custom_components/hungry_machines/__init__.py custom_components/hungry_machines/auth.py custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py` returns 0 outside of the config_flow.py form definition (assert the password is not stored in entry.data — search entry.data writes for 'password' should yield nothing in __init__.py / auth.py / scheduler.py).

stories.title: Type sweep + shared hourly-expansion helper + override guards
stories.description: Historical — completed in the previous batch. Kept here for reference; priority 99 so RALPH skips it.
acceptanceCriteria: (See git history for the full criteria — story is complete and all checks passed.)
stories.notes: Created src/utils/hourly.ts exporting expandHourlyTo48 (with RangeError on wrong length), collapse48ToHourly (inverse, samples even indices, RangeError on wrong length), hasHourlyComfortBands (length-24 guard), and hasCustomRates (source==='custom'). Tightened HvacScheduleResponse.source, ApplianceScheduleEntry.source, and ApplianceSchedule.source to literal union 'optimization' | 'defaults' (RatesResponse.source already 'custom' | 'zone'). Refactored constraint-editor._seedHourlyBands to call hasHourlyComfortBands. Refactored panel custom-rates section to call hasCustomRates(rates). Added tests/hourly.test.ts with 14 cases. All 67 frontend tests pass; tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build green.

stories.title: Schedule chart shows user comfort bands as translucent overlay
stories.description: Historical — completed in the previous batch. Kept here for reference; priority 99 so RALPH skips it.
acceptanceCriteria: (See git history for the full criteria — story is complete and all checks passed.)
stories.notes: Extended hm-schedule-chart with optional comfortHighs/comfortLows props. Renders translucent comfort-band <rect>s in amber BEFORE the existing temp-band rects. Panel side: fetches /api/v1/preferences in parallel with schedules/rates/appliances, gracefully degrades on 500. Added a small legend below the chart only when comfort arrays exist. Tests extended in schedule-chart.test.ts and panel-schedules.test.ts. All 73 frontend tests pass; tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build green.
